channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: ICE acknowledges it is using powerful spyware
item.description: In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.
item.content:encoded:
In a letter sent last week, ICE's top official indicated to members of Congress the agency is using a spyware tool to intercept encrypted messages of fentanyl traffickers.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: In a stark reversal, President Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran
item.description: President Trump has announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on their opening of the Strait of Hormuz. In an earlier online post, he had threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight."
item.content:encoded: President Trump has announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, contingent on their opening of the Strait of Hormuz. In an earlier online post, he had threatened "a whole civilization will die tonight."
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Astronauts suggest naming a moon crater 'Carroll' after their commander's late wife
item.description: The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in 2020.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II crew, led by Reid Wiseman, was the first to lay eyes on several craters on the far side of the moon. The astronauts want to name one of them after Carroll Wiseman, who died of cancer in 2020.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire, suspending Trump's threat to annihilate Iran
item.description: After Iran ceded to President Trump's demand to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump backed down from his threat of wide-scale destruction of Iran's civilian and military infrastructure.
item.content:encoded:
After Iran ceded to President Trump's demand to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump backed down from his threat of wide-scale destruction of Iran's civilian and military infrastructure.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse
item.description: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.content:encoded: The NASA moon mission completed several key milestones as its crew looped around the lunar body. It's expected to splash down on Earth on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Nearly 50 years later, Cincinnati may get a real 'WKRP'
item.description: Nearly 50 years after a sitcom made the name famous, it appears there will actually be a "WKRP in Cincinnati."
item.content:encoded: Nearly 50 years after a sitcom made the name famous, it appears there will actually be a "WKRP in Cincinnati."
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: JP Morgan CEO talks about the economy, the Iran war and AI
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon about the economy, the impact of the war in Iran and artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon about the economy, the impact of the war in Iran and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Michigan muscles its way to program's 2nd NCAA basketball title, beating UConn
item.description: High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.
item.content:encoded:
High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.

(Image credit: Michael Conroy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How a free pop-up health clinic in Texas is reaching a community of fishermen
item.description: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.content:encoded: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Myong

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
item.description: Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.
item.content:encoded:
Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg
